destinations.evidence: RitualNode selects the custom RitualExecutorBuilder in its component builder.
The executor builder wraps the upstream Ethereum EVM config with RitualEvmFactory and returns a RitualBlockExecutorProvider.

equivalence_argument: The nodebuilder dependency owns this node-construction invariant: RitualNode routes block execution through RitualExecutorBuilder and a delegating RitualBlockExecutorProvider while preserving Ethereum components around it.
rationale: The primary diff replaces EthereumExecutorBuilder with RitualExecutorBuilder in RitualNode's component builder and type aliases, then implements RitualEvmConfig as a wrapper over EthEvmConfig and RitualBlockExecutor as a delegating wrapper over EthBlockExecutor. That suggests the intended behavior is to install a Ritual-owned execution extension point without changing ordinary Ethereum execution behavior in this commit. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: After this commit, RitualNode block execution should be built through RitualExecutorBuilder/RitualEvmConfig while preserving Ethereum block execution semantics delegated to the upstream EthBlockExecutor and EthEvmConfig.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate a RitualNode after this commit and execute a block containing normal Ethereum transactions against the same chain spec with both the previous Ethereum executor path and the new Ritual executor path; fail if the Ritual path is not selected or if receipts, gas used, state root, or execution errors differ for the same inputs.
summary: RitualNode execution goes through the custom executor wrapper in nodebuilder.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate RitualNode::components and execute a normal Ethereum block through the Ritual executor path, comparing receipts/state/gas with the Ethereum executor for unchanged semantics.
title: Ritual node execution goes through a custom executor wrapper
